annotation.speech2: The graph is a rational function separated into three branches by the vertical asymptotes at x=-1 and x=3. The graph has a horizontal asymptote at y=4. The graph passes through the origin (0,0) which is both the x-intercept and y-intercept. For x less than -1, the graph is below the x-axis and approaches the vertical asymptote at x=-1 from below and approaches the horizontal asymptote at y=4 from below. For -1 less than x less than 3, the graph is above the x-axis and approaches the vertical asymptote at x=-1 from above and approaches the vertical asymptote at x=3 from above. For x greater than 3, the graph is above the x-axis and approaches the vertical asymptote at x=3 from above and approaches the horizontal asymptote at y=4 from above.
